mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is driven by labor force participation changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle indicate supply-side bottlenecks that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations are anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 4.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, which increase discount rates and compress equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%, or the predictive coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k AND UNRATE declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which correlates with broader economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with HOUST levels after adjusting for interest rate trends.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals resolving supply chain constraints and improving corporate margins, leading to immediate positive equity market reactions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power when global commodity prices decline simultaneously.
